title: Normie mode does not append turns to self.messages — continuity broken on mode switch back to root
what_failed: Normie mode wrote conversation turns only to self.history (a string-only research-mode helper) and skipped self.messages entirely. When mode flipped back to root, Claude received a message list missing every normie turn, so the session looked brand new from the API's perspective.
where_failed: pi_agent.py _respond_normie() — only self.history.append(...) was called; self.messages was never updated.
why_likely: Two parallel stores (self.messages for Claude, self.history for research-mode strings) drifted. Normie wrote to one, root read from the other.
reproduction: In normie mode, share several facts. Switch to root. Ask Pi to repeat one of the facts. Pi replies as if it had never been told.
expected: Both modes write to a unified self.messages so the conversation reads as one thread regardless of which model produced any given turn.
actual: Continuity hard-resets on mode switch. The L-001 invariant ('mode is not a session boundary') was violated indirectly by the divergence.
suggested_fix: Append both user and assistant turns to self.messages in _respond_normie(). Reuse the safe truncation helper to keep history bounded.
